item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect mix of humor and a really good scp article, I loved the interview and this is just generally a funny, unique and well written article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My guess: Probably because the dodos are intelligent and uncooperative. The one interviewed dodo already hates humans and sees us as inferior life forms. And the foundation doesn't know how their reality-bending powers worked, but one could randomly be born with residual memories of how to use them.

Actually, I think we'd be better off with SCP-3758-B. Actually, wait, scratch that. SCP-3758-B's memories are intact. They might remember how the god-medicine worked.

So it's the reality-bending. That's why they're keter, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't certain about this article until the audio files, all of which were absolute gold.

I'm still taller than you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this Keter? It seems like it usually stays in its box as long as you follow procedures, and even the "worst case" scenario shown, a "walking Chernobyl", is easily contained (from a Foundation PoV; you plop enough concrete around it and you're fine). Suggest change to Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Henderson: And why did you abandon—
PoI-3758-27: 'cos he's a prick.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a lovely dodo SCP, +1 for the fun execution along with sentient birds. It was fun reading the interview with the snarky bird, ah yes weighing more than someone is commendable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Intelligent dinosaurs that could've established advanced civilization before the yetis! They say the extinction event paved way for civilization but who knew it was really like this. lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome, I loved how we end up feeling bad for the ancient dodo martyr. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a wild ride, every second of it. This is amazing, and I upvoted before I even finished the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: we finally have the dado skip we deserve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ll always upvote a skip that takes a rediculous idea and turns it into a well-executed experience for the reader. Everybody was really well personified in a short amount of space, and we finally have the dado skip we deserve.

+1

item.title: Oh No You Dodon't
item.content:encoded: Yes. Just all the yes. This is such a fun SCP, and I adore it for the sheer amount of silliness it packs. This is the legacy dodos deserve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the pure quill*. I laughed out loud, in a very real and legally binding sense. This started off well and just went from there. Have you read "The Ugly Chickens" by Howard Waldrop? It also uses everybody's favorite extinct bird, but in a very different way.

*Victorian era term for authentic item or product. From cinchona bark, source of quinine. If what you had was a roll of bark, you had a pure quill; anything more processed could have been adultered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wait i was typing the same comment
I've had a dodo article in my sandbox for months :'(

Still +1 anyway, this is a fine article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Fuck you)2 for doing a dodo skip before me, and for doing it better than I ever could have.

I was all set to upvote by the end of the description, but it maintained cruising speed the whole way through. Creative anomaly, fun character to hear in my mind's ear, easy +1.

item.title: obscure looney tunes reference goes here.
item.content:encoded:
Regarding image rights:

The dodo image is public domain.
The cartouche is my own illustration.
